data.title: Mersana Therapeutics to execute 1-for-25 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Mersana Therapeutics (NASDAQ:MRSN) plans to implement a 1-for-25 reverse stock split of the issued shares of the company’s common stock, effective July 25, 2025.
  • The stock split is intended at increasing the per-share market price of the company’s common stock to enable the company to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement for continued listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market.
  • The action will reduce the number of outstanding shares of Mersana’s common stock from ~124.8 million shares to ~5.0 million shares.
  • MRSN --8.7% premarket to $0.37
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Eaton adds solar project in Wisconsin to power regional manufacturing
data.description:
Power management company Eaton (NYSE:ETN) on Thursday said that it would add a new clean energy project to drive carbon reduction at its operations in Wisconsin, advancing its commitment to achieve net zero operations by 2050.

The joint project with We Energies, a subsidiary of WEC Energy (WEC), and Eaton will help strengthen energy security, deliver cost-effective power to the grid, and reduce Eaton’s carbon footprint at five locations in Wisconsin.

The 4 MW installation is expected to offset 30% carbon generation across five Eaton facilities and accelerate progress toward the company's sustainability goals.

data.title: SolarBank to rebrand to PowerBank
data.description:
• SolarBank (NASDAQ:SUUN) has announced it will change its name to PowerBank Corporation, reflecting its broader strategic focus and growing role as a provider of comprehensive power and energy solutions beyond solar.
  • The effective date for the name change is July 28, 2025. Stock symbols will remain the same.

data.title: Iron ore exports rise to monthly record at Australia's Port Hedland
data.description:
Iron ore prices trade lower Thursday with signs of stronger supply, as iron ore shipments from Port Hedland, Australia's largest bulk export terminal, swelled to a record in June.

Exports from Port Hedland rose to 54.6M last month, exceeding a peak set a year ago, and YTD cargoes increased to ~288M tons, edging past the previous half-year record of 286.9M tons set last year, according to Pilbara Ports Authority data.

The Port Hedland complex in Western Australia handles cargoes for producers including BHP (NYSE:BHP) and Fortescue (OTCQX:FSUMF), which reported record annual iron ore shipments of 198.4M tons in the 12 months through June, up 4% Y/Y.

On Thursday, Fortescue (OTCQX:FSUMF) also said it will not proceed with green hydrogen projects in Arizona and Gladstone, Australia.

Analysts at Jarden praised the strong performance of Fortescue’s (OTCQX:FSUMF) iron ore operations, as "the standout performance for us was in cost control," as the miner's production expenses declined by 1% in its first annual reduction in costs since FY 2020.

Vale (NYSE:VALE) said earlier this week it produced 83.6M tons of iron ore in Q2, the company's highest Q2 output since 2021.

Sentiment in the iron ore market nevertheless remains broadly positive following the Chinese government's announcement that it will proceed with a 1.2T yuan hydropower project, which could provide a massive boost to the steel and concrete sectors, ANZ Research analysts wrote.

The most actively traded September iron ore contract (SCO:COM) on the Dalian Commodity Exchange recently traded -0.7% at 809.5 yuan/ton.

data.title: XXIX Metal announces $6 million financing
data.description:
• XXIX Metal (TSXV:XXIX:CA) announced on Thursday that it has entered into an agreement with Beacon Securities Limited to act as lead agent and book runner for private placement offering of up to 24.8M Ontario charity flow-through units at $0.121 per unit and up to 22.73M Québec charity flow-through units at $0.132 per unit for combined gross proceeds of up to $6M.
  • In connection with the offering, the company has granted the Agents an option to sell up to an additional number of 16.53 million Ontario FT Units at the Ontario FT Issue Price and up to 15.16 million Québec FT Units at the Québec Issue Price.
  • The company will use an amount equal to the gross proceeds from the sale of the offered securities to incur eligible Canadian exploration expenses.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about August 14, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AstraZeneca succeeds in late-stage trial for myasthenia gravis therapy
data.description:
AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) announced Thursday that gefurulimab, its experimental injectable for generalized myasthenia gravis, reached the main goals in a Phase 3 trial for the rare autoimmune disorder.

Citing data from its PREVAIL Phase 3 trial, the Anglo-Swedish drugmaker said its once-weekly treatment, compared to placebo, led to a statistically significant and clinically meaningful improvement over 26 weeks in a patient-reported scale designed to assess gMG signs and symptoms.

The 260-subject global trial enrolled adults with anti-acetylcholine receptor antibody-positive gMG, with its primary endpoint being the change in the Myasthenia Gravis Activities of Daily Living (MG-ADL) total score from baseline.

PREVAIL achieved its primary and all secondary endpoints, and gefurulimab was well-tolerated among patients with no new safety signals, the company added.

Astra (NASDAQ:AZN) plans to present the results at a future medical event and share the findings with global regulators, while an open-label extension phase of the trial is currently underway for patients who have completed the randomized phase.

One of the leading players in the gMG market includes argenx (ARGX). The Dutch drugmaker markets its neonatal Fc receptor blocker, Vyvgart, with the Chinese biotech Zai Lab (ZLAB) globally for gMG, competing with AstraZeneca’s (NASDAQ:AZN) rival product, Soliris, a complement inhibitor.

data.title: American Eagle soars after deal with Sydney Sweeney and Reddit buzz
data.description:
American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO) shot up in premarket trading on Thursday on high volume after announcing a new high-profile campaign.

The company said it is honoring its denim heritage with the launch of the American Eagle (AE) brand's Fall 2025 campaign, headlined by Sydney Sweeney. The actress is seen elevating AEO's position as the top jeans brand for Generation Z. As part of the campaign, the retailer will launch "The Sydney Jean," a limited-run take on their fan-favorite Dreamy Drape franchise, produced in collaboration with Sweeney.

Of note, American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO) has also seen a jump in social media mentions since the market closed on Wednesday, including some special attention from the WallStreetBets crowd on Reddit (RDDT).

Shares of American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) were up 18.5% in premarket trading at 8:20 a.m. after being up a high as 25%. Short interest on AEO stands at 12.2% of the total float.

data.title: DEFSEC Technologies prices C$6.8M securities offering
data.description:
• DEFSEC Technologies (NASDAQ:DFSC) has priced its offering of 759,879 common shares, or pre-funded warrants in lieu of shares, bundled with common share purchase warrants to buy up to 759,879 additional shares at C$8.955 per share and accompanying warrant.
  • The warrants will have an exercise price of C$10.52 per share, are exercisable upon issuance and will expire five years following the date of issuance.
  • The offering is expected to generate gross proceeds of approximately C$6.8 million, which DEFSEC plans to use for working capital and general corporate purposes.
